v1: Calls from GNM Pressure Washing or properties they manage (Chevron and SOs gas stations) for emergencies after hours.

v2.criteria: Caller identifies as GNM Pressure Washing or mentions properties managed by GNM Pressure Washing (e.g., Chevron, SOs gas stations).

changes.v1: Initially, calls will be conditionally forwarded to Clara if Ben does not answer or declines on his main number. Once Ben's second number is active, Clara will be the first point of contact and will transfer calls to that second number if a direct conversation with Ben is requested.
changes.v2: During business hours, calls initially go to Ben's main line. If unanswered or declined, they forward to Clara. Once Ben's second number is active, Clara will be the first point of contact and can transfer calls to Ben's second number upon request for non-emergency situations.

changes.v1: For GNM Pressure Washing and properties they manage, emergency calls are patched through to Ben. For all other callers after hours, Clara will state that she will get back to them on the next business day.

Clara will answer calls, provide information, and transfer to Ben's second number if a caller wishes to speak directly to him. Clara should mention the service call fee details ($115 call-out fee covering the first 30 minutes, and $98/hour for residential work in 30-minute increments) only if explicitly asked by the caller.

changes.v1: Email for post-call notifications: info@bendselectricsolutionsteam.com. The agent has been pre-trained using information from the company's website. A follow-up meeting is scheduled for Friday at 2:00 PM (local time, ~15-20 min duration) to review test calls and dashboard features.
changes.v2: When asked, Clara should mention the service call pricing structure: $115 call-out fee and $98/hour ($49/half-hour) residential rate. Clara should not proactively mention these fees unless specifically asked by the caller. Email for post-call notifications: info@Ben's Electric Solutions Team.com. SMS for post-call notifications will be sent to Ben's main business line.
